channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Chronos vs Toto: Zero-Shot Forecasting Benchmark Results
item.description: Introduction Good forecasts help with capacity planning and quieter alerts. But one traffic spike or memory leak can make any forecast useless. The goal is simple: prove your forecast beats a...

item.title: Edge-Cached Localhost Tunnels: How to Give Stakeholders a Production-Fast Preview Directly from Your IDE
item.description: IT InstaTunnel Team Published by our engineering team Edge-Cached Localhost Tunnels: How to Give Stakeholders a Production-Fast Preview Directly from Your IDE There is a specific kind of pain that...

item.title: Radiation-Proof Flash Storage Could Be the Missing Layer for AI Data Centers in Space
item.description: AI is forcing us to rethink the physical limits of computing. On Earth, data centers are running into familiar constraints: power availability, cooling, land, water usage, permitting, and grid...

item.title: AI Learning Roadmap: Where to Start if You're a Complete Beginner
item.description: Nowadays, AI is everywhere. More than ever, people want to learn but being the internet flooded with resources makes it incredibly hard to know where to start. It feels like there is too much...

item.title: I built 6 free dev tools to skip the signup walls — here's what I learned
item.description: You know that moment when you just want to decode a JWT, but jwt.io wants you to log in to "save your tokens"? Or when you need a quick curl command and Postman's 200MB Electron app feels like...

item.title: How to Set Realistic Goals for an Open Source Project?
item.description: “Without clear goals, projects can slowly lose focus over time.” Lately I’ve been thinking a lot about what the right goals for an open source project should actually look like. Because I think...

item.title: How I Built an Indonesian NLP Parser That Understands Warung Owners, Then Abandoned It
item.description: This is a submission for the GitHub Finish-Up-A-Thon Challenge What I Built Warung MiMo is an AI-powered assistant for small warungs (Indonesian street shops). It lets shop owners manage...

item.title: Keyboard shortcuts that fixed my editing flow
item.description: I used to lose my train of thought every time I reached for the mouse to translate or fix a sentence. It sounds minor, but when you're writing in a second language and constantly switching between...

item.title: I Built an AI-Native Productivity System Instead of Another AI Wrapper
item.description: Most productivity apps today feel passive. They organize tasks. Track deadlines. Store notes. But they rarely help people actually execute. That idea became the starting point for Momentum AI — an...

item.title: LogicNodes MCP bridge: Connecting Claude to real-world utility
item.description: --- title: "LogicNodes MCP Bridge: Connecting Claude to Real-World Utility" description: "Discover how LogicNodes' Multi-Chain Protocol (MCP) bridge empowers AI models like Claude to interact with...

item.title: Did the Pope use AI to write about the dangers of AI?
item.description: It's possible that AI was used to write parts of Pope Leo XIV's latest encyclical about AI's impact on humanity. An analysis by Linch Zhang posted on the forum LessWrong found certain paragraphs...

item.title: Pope Leo Schooled the Tech Bros on Tolkien
item.description: The Holy Father referenced The Lord of the Rings in his encyclical about AI—an expert (if unintentional) troll of tech billionaires who keep misinterpreting the series.

item.title: DuckDuckGo installs are up 30% as users reject being ‘force-fed’ Google’s AI Search
item.description: Google overhauled Search at I/O 2026, replacing blue links with AI agents. The backlash has been swift. DuckDuckGo app installs spiked 30% as users seek a way out.

item.title: NASA’s permanent Moon base plans start with three missions this year
item.description: On Tuesday, NASA announced several upcoming lunar missions to the Moon's South Pole region. These missions will pave the way for the crewed Artemis landing slated for 2028, starting with three...

item.title: Why the Vatican Invited Anthropic to the Pope’s AI Encyclical Presentation
item.description: Pope Leo’s first encyclical marks an unprecedented alliance between the Church and Silicon Valley.

item.title: What Pope Leo XIV’s First Encyclical Says About the Power of AI
item.description: In Magnifica Humanitas, the Pope decries the concentration of technological power in a few global players.

item.title: Millions of AI agents imperiled by critical vulnerability in open source package
item.description: "BadHost" was found in Starlette, a package with 325 million weekly downloads.

item.title: Google Health is here, but a lot of people want their Fitbit app back instead
item.description: The Fitbit app is no more. Along with the launch of the new Fitbit Air (which you can expect a full review of once we've spent more time with it), Google has officially replaced it with Google...

item.title: GE’s nugget ice maker is nearly half off if you buy it refurbished
item.description: If you’re looking for ways to make staying cool and hydrated easier this summer, Woot is currently selling a refurbished GE Profile Opal 2.0 Ultra Nugget Ice Maker for $264.99 through June 3rd....

item.title: OpenRouter more than doubles valuation to $1.3B in a year
item.description: OpenRouter has raised a $113 million Series B led by CapitalG. Its 5x growth in usage over six months indicates the multi-AI-model future is here.

item.title: Sony’s sloppy Spider-Man universe gets even messier with Spider-Noir
item.description: After years of it seeming like the Spider-Man film rights might be better off in Marvel's hands alone, Into the Spider-Verse came along and proved that Sony was still capable of telling phenomenal...

item.title: The best Memorial Day sales you can still shop
item.description: The long weekend is over, but many of the best Memorial Day deals are still available. Many of our favorite portable speakers are discounted, as are other outdoorsy products we like, including a...

item.title: American Airlines is getting Starlink Wi-Fi
item.description: American Airlines is planning to install SpaceX's Starlink Wi-Fi in hundreds of its airplanes, the airline announced today. American says the deployment will start in the first quarter of 2027,...

item.title: This startup is betting India’s gig economy can train the world’s robots
item.description: Human Archive, a startup founded by UC Berkeley and Stanford researchers, is paying gig workers in India to wear camera-equipped caps and sensor devices to collect the real-world physical training...

item.title: Memory V re-creates the Memorymoog without the massive headaches or price tag
item.description: The Memorymoog is legendary for being an absolutely massive-sounding synth and being incredibly unreliable. But now you can enjoy its classic Moog sound without the headaches or the sky-high...

item.title: MuddyWater Uses DLL Side-Loading in Espionage Campaign Targeting 9 Countries
item.description: The Iranian hacking group known as MuddyWater has been linked to a new campaign affecting at least nine organizations across nine countries on four continents in the first quarter of 2026. The...

item.title: Saving for a Switch 2 is easier with Newegg’s gift card deal
item.description: If you foresee a bunch of Nintendo purchases in your future, Newegg’s deal on Nintendo gift cards might just help save your wallet. The retailer is offering a deal that lets gamers get $200 worth...

item.title: Identifying People Using Wi-Fi Routers
item.description: Not identifying people based on their use of Wi-Fi routers, but identifying people using Wi-Fi signals. This is accomplished through what is known as WiFi sensing, or the use of WiFi signals to...

item.title: Universal Music Group and TikTok renew agreement to combat unauthorized AI music
item.description: For years, UMG has pushed platforms, streaming services, and AI companies to implement stricter content moderation policies.

item.title: Rethinking organizational design in the age of agentic AI
item.description: Amid rapidly growing adoption of enterprise-level AI agents, there’s a disconnect emerging between ambition and execution. Although 85% of organizations say they want to be agentic within the next...
